title: Dead Code, Unused Dependencies, and Reachability · Policy Clinic: Dependency Analysis
summary: Remove unreachable code and dependencies with evidence rather than guesswork. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gates dependency analysis increment in a dead-code and dependency audit. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Detect unused, missing, duplicate, and undeclared dependencies.
Detect unreachable branches, impossible states, and obsolete feature paths.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A package is needed whenever it appears in a lockfile. This fails the Dependency Analysis contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Dependency Analysis evidence
steps.instruction: Append a gates-dependency-analysis-1rr5cl: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Dependency Analysis into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-dependency-analysis-1rr5cl:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
